column2: The global population growth since 1700 is staggering. We have grown a lot over the past 325 years because of healthcare, economy, and evolution. The world population is now at 8 billion, the highest it has ever been.
column3: The global population has grown exponentially and faster than it ever has.

column2: The growth rate compared to the previous 10,000 years is very staggering. We have grown by billions in the last 300 years because of all of the advancements we have made as a world. The growth rate in the previous 10,000 years has been low because there hasn't been any healthcare and nutritious food, which has contributed to a lot of deaths in the previous 10,000 years.
column3: The growth rate now is significantly faster than the last 10,000 years.

column2: The Demographic Transition Model is a model used to show the development of a country, with 4 stages. They each represent a level of development in a country. For example, Stage 1, with a high birth rate and high death rate, represents a developing country due to a young population and not having sufficient healthcare or food.
column3: The demographic transition model shows a pattern that most developed countries have followed. In stage 1, population is low from high death rates. In stage 2, countries have an extremely high growth rate. In stage 3, the birth rate declines but because of their young population, the overall population continues to grow. In stage 4, population growth slows.

column2: The role of women in population growth rate is substantial. This is because they are the bearer of the child and they can decide how many kids they want. If a women wants a lot of kids, then they can and that would increase the population growth rate.
column3: A women's education level and rights determines the population growth rate in a country. In countries where women have access to education and a career, many women choose to have less kids or no kids at all because they are busy with their job. In countries where women have limited access to education, birth rates are higher.

column2: The results of China's one-child policy were staggering. It caused population growth to decline because the government saw that there were too many people and wanted to slow the population growth. The consequences of this were that population growth declined and people worked more because the government mandated that each family could only have one child.
column3: Due to China's one-child policy, China now has an aging population and a high dependency ratio. Their population growth rate has slowed, and they are now implementing natalist policies. Additionally, chinese culture prefers boys to girls, leading to a disproportionate boy to girl ratio.

column2:
1. France - They are discouraging people to have children and that has caused a high dependency ratio because there are less working-age people supporting more elderly people which has also caused higher healthcare costs due to medicines and treatments becoming high in demand.
2. Germany - They have a lot of people who are working in factories and that has caused many people to not have children because they work until they are past their ability to have children. This has also caused a spike in healthcare costs because you have more elderly people being supported by less working-age people.
3. Ukraine - They have had a lot of warfare going on because Russia is claiming their territory but Ukraine say that they are a free nation. This has caused a lot of people to migrate and not have jobs. Their land is destroyed, so when the war is over, they have to completely rebuild cities and other places which could increase prices that people have to pay so that buildings get rebuilt.
column3: The U.S, Denmark, and France are all aging populations with an average age above 45. Aging populations occur

column2: Food production is the amount of food produced, and food availability is how accessible the food produced is.
column3: Malthusian theory, the theory that food production will not be able to keep up with population growth, leading to famine, has not happened. Despite this, food insecurity and malnutrition are common in many countries. This is because the problem isn't that there isn't enough food, it's how the food is distributed. Some countries have more than enough food, while food is scarce in others. Developed countries have better technology, leading to more efficient agriculture.

column2: A primate city is a type of city that is the top rank in a hierarchy.
column3: The most important and influential city within a country.

column2:
New York City - United States
Tokyo - Japan
column3: Seoul, South Korea and London, U.K are examples of primate cities and their respective countries.

column2: Primate cities exist because they are a central hub in industrial or economic activity and are needed in national interaction.
column3: Primate cities exist to serve as the cultural and financial center of their country.

column2: Some countries lack primate cities because they don't have a central place for world influence.
column3: Some countries are not developed enough to have primate cities, and some have multiple influential cities like the U.S.

column2: World cities are places that facilitate global interaction; primate cities are top cities in a hierarchy
column3: World cities are cities that significantly impact global culture. They are distinct from primate cities because primate cities only need to be influential within their own country.

column2: A megacity is a city that is a global center for interaction; primate cities are cities that are a central hub for the country.
column3: Megacities are extremely large cities with a population of at least 10 million. This requirement only accounts for population, and not the influence of the city. Because of this, some megacities are also primate cities, while some aren't.

column2:
New York City - It's a global trade center that has influence on many countries around the world
Los Angeles - It's a port city that imports packages from all over the world.
column3: New York and Los Angeles are two cities in the US that are also world cities. They are both world cities because they influence global culture. For example, both are often mentioned in movies, even if the movie wasn't made in the U.S.

column2: A multinational state is a state that has many diverse nations included.
column3: A state made up of multiple nations.

column2: Centrifugal forces are factors that make people want to leave a place, such as violence or low wages. Centripetal forces are factors that entice people into moving to a country, such as better wages, housing, etc.
column3: Centripetal forces are forces that unite a population, such as a shared language, while a centrifugal force is a force that divides a population, such as having different religions.

column2: Language, culture and religion can all act as centrifugal or centripetal forces. Language can help unite people or it can act as a deterring factor for leaving places. Culture can act as a shared place for people to meet, talk, and have shared feelings. Culture can also be a centrifugal force if people don't agree with what customs or traditions a culture does. Religion can be a shared sense of belonging with people, contributing to a centripetal force. Religion can also start fights, conflicts, and protests which could make people not want to join that religion, contributing to a centrifugal force.
column3: Having a shared language, culture, or religion acts as a centripetal force and brings people together over shared traits. Having a different language, culture, or religion can create conflict, acting as a centrifugal force.

column2: Colonialism has had a major role in shaping the religion, customs, and language of countries. Many colonies took over countries in Africa, Asia, and South America, which contributed to those countries using languages that the colonies imposed on these captured territories.
column3: Colonialism acts as a centrifugal force, dividing borders without knowledge of the area's cultures.

column2: The imagined communities concept was made to predict what communities would be like in different countries in the future.
The concept applies to Indonesia because the concept shaped how these people in these communities in Indonesia act.

Indonesia is vulnerable to separatist movements because these movements can make propaganda that people believe and agree with to where they start protests and fights to overthrow governments to have their idea be used across the country.
column3: Indonesia is a multinational state and one of world's largest and most diverse countries. Because of this, factors such as different languages, cultures, or religions act as a centrifugal force, dividing the population and leading to separatist movements.
